summary: Iteration 37 — Phase 5a (Reply-with-template post-send fixes) verified end-to-end. Backend pytest: 10/10 PASS. Frontend Playwright: 5/5 critical flows PASS. (1) Reply-To fallback chain correctly prefers template.default_from (paul@creativemojo.co.uk) over logged-in admin — verified via source-precedence regex AND template default_from check; we did NOT call /api/email/send-reply to avoid real Resend billing as instructed. (2) POST /api/email/sends/{id}/mark-replied appends a {type:'replied',marked_by:...} event, flips last_event to 'replied', is idempotent (second call returns already_marked:true, no dup event), and DELETE removes the marker and recomputes last_event. (3) Temperature engine: GET /api/contacts/{id}/temperature includes a 'Marked as replied' bucket {count:0,weight:15,max:15}; marking lifts score by exactly +15, undoing drops it back. (4) Frontend: 13 synthetic Resend BCC-duplicate events (Sent×4, Delivered×3, Opened×3, Clicked×3) collapsed to 4 deduped rows with ×N badges in the [data-testid='email-timeline'] expand-pane. (5) Green 'Mark as Replied' button (testid 'mark-replied-<sendId>') visible; click swaps to grey 'Undo Replied' (testid 'unmark-replied-<sendId>'); top badge flips CLICKED→REPLIED (EVENT_META.replied / Reply icon / emerald). Regression: /admin/email-templates and admin /landing-pages routes still 200.

verified_items.evidence: test_iter37_reply_to_dedupe.py::TestReplyToFallback — Franchise Enquiry Reply template (id 8ebca28f-…) has default_from='paul@creativemojo.co.uk'. Source regex confirms `if template_reply_to: reply_to = template_reply_to else: reply_to = user.email` ordering at resend_routes.py:237-240. Existing email_sends doc (pre-fix) has reply_to='admin@creativemojo.co.uk' but next send will route to paul@. Actual send NOT executed (Resend billed).

First POST returns {ok:true, event:{type:'replied',at:..,marked_by:'admin@creativemojo.co.uk'}}; last_event flips to 'replied'. Second POST returns {ok:true, already_marked:true} and events array still has exactly 1 replied event. DELETE returns {ok:true} and last_event recomputes back to 'sent'. 404 on unknown send_id.
Baseline score X → after mark, score = X+15 (pytest.approx). 'Marked as replied' bucket count flips 0→1 with weight=15, max=15. Undo restores baseline.

Injected 13 synthetic events (Sent×4 @10:00:00, Delivered×3 @10:00:15, Opened×3 @10:00:30, Clicked×3 @10:00:45 same link). Expanded timeline shows exactly 4 rows: 'Sent ×4', 'Delivered ×3', 'Opened ×3', 'Clicked ×3' + link. Mongo restored to single sent event post-test.

Green button [data-testid='mark-replied-fb8ed962-…'] visible in expanded row. Click → button swaps to [data-testid='unmark-replied-…']. Top-level row badge changes from 'CLICKED' to 'REPLIED' (emerald Reply icon — EVENT_META.replied).
Drawer header shows 'WARM · 10.5 AUTO' for Joana Matias post-engagement — proves /api/contacts/{id}/temperature is wired and pulling cached/live engagement counts.
GET /api/email-templates returns 2 templates incl. default_from='paul@…' for both.

critical_code_review_comments: resend_routes.py:264 — `resp = await asyncio.to_thread(resend.Emails.send, params)` is correct, but if Resend returns {error:..., id:None} (e.g. quota exceeded) the route still inserts an email_sends doc with resend_id=None and returns 200. Consider: assert resp.get('id') after the call and 502 if missing, otherwise a 'sent' event is logged for a message that never left. Low priority but masks delivery failures.

resend_routes.py:316-333 — mark_replied looks up `events` first then issues a $push, which creates a small race window if two admins click Mark-as-Replied simultaneously (both pass the idempotency check, both push). Use $addToSet-by-projection or a unique compound on (id,event_type) to harden — but in practice only one admin uses the modal so this is theoretical.

EmailTimeline.jsx:152 — `s.events.length > 1` gates the entire Activity section; means a single-event send (sent only) hides the Mark-as-Replied affordance from the Activity ladder but the button still appears above. UX is fine, just noting the threshold.

EmailTimeline.jsx:163 — `Math.floor(new Date(e.at).getTime() / 10000)` buckets to 10-second windows, BUT events crossing a bucket boundary (e.g. 10:00:09 vs 10:00:10) won't collapse. For Resend's burst webhooks this is essentially safe (all fire within ~1s), but worth documenting. Consider rounding to the bucket the FIRST event of that type/link lands in (sticky bucket) for theoretical robustness.
seed_data_creation: Temporarily injected 13 synthetic events into email_sends doc fb8ed962-…  (Sent×4 / Delivered×3 / Opened×3 / Clicked×3) to verify the dedupe IIFE. Restored to original single 'sent' event after frontend run. Mark-replied markers added during pytest cleaned up by the autouse _reset_marker fixture (DELETE before+after each test).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three reported issues (reply-to fallback, timeline dedupe, manual mark-as-replied) are working. /app/backend/tests/test_iter37_reply_to_dedupe.py is the regression suite — re-run whenever resend_routes.py or EmailTimeline.jsx is touched. Note: we did NOT actually call /api/email/send-reply (Resend billed) — reply-to logic is verified by source-regex + template default_from. If a future change refactors the reply_to selection out of the inline route, add a unit-test that imports the helper and asserts the precedence directly. The synthetic 13-event injection trick (cd /app/backend && set -a; source .env; set +a; python3 motor script — see iter_37 shell history) is the cleanest way to visually verify dedupe — always restore after.
rca of the issue: N/A — Phase 5a implementation verified working end-to-end. Three reported user issues are resolved: (a) template.default_from now drives reply_to so replies land in paul@creativemojo.co.uk not admin@; (b) duplicate Resend BCC webhook events collapse to ×N badges (type+link+10s bucket); (c) manual Mark-as-Replied button persists a {type:'replied'} event idempotently with +15 lead-temperature lift and a one-click Undo.
